children.text: the term “covered loan” means a loan guaranteed under ;section 636(a)(36) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is a liability of the borrower;

children.label: B
children.text: is a mortgage on real or personal property; and

children.label: C
children.text: was incurred before ;February 15, 2020

the term “covered operations expenditure” means a payment for any business software or cloud computing service that facilitates business operations, product or service delivery, the processing, payment, or tracking of payroll expenses, human resources, sales and billing functions, or accounting or tracking of supplies, inventory, records and expenses;

children.label: A
children.text: beginning on the date of the origination of a covered loan; and

children.label: i
children.text: beginning on the date that is 8 weeks after such date of origination; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ending on the date that is 24 weeks after such date of origination;

the term “covered property damage cost” means a cost related to property damage and vandalism or looting due to public disturbances that occurred during 2020 that was not covered by insurance or other compensation;
the term “covered rent obligation” means rent obligated under a leasing agreement in force before ;February 15, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: are essential to the operations of the entity at the time at which the expenditure is made; and

children.label: i
children.text: in effect at any time before the covered period with respect to the applicable covered loan; or

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to perishable goods, in effect before or at any time during the covered period with respect to the applicable covered loan;

the term “covered utility payment” means payment for a service for the distribution of electricity, gas, water, transportation, telephone, or internet access for which service began before ;February 15, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: means an operating or a capital expenditure to facilitate the adaptation of the business activities of an entity to comply with requirements established or guidance issued by the Department of Health and Human Services, the Centers for Disease Control, or the Occupational Safety and Health Administration, or any equivalent requirements established or guidance issued by a State or local government, during the period beginning on and ending the date on which the national emergency declared by the President under the National Emergencies Act ( et seq.) with respect to the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID–19) expires related to the maintenance of standards for sanitation, social distancing, or any other worker or customer safety requirement related to COVID–19;March 1, 202050 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: I
children.text: a drive-through window facility;

children.label: II
children.text: an indoor, outdoor, or combined air or air pressure ventilation or filtration system;

children.label: III
children.text: a physical barrier such as a sneeze guard;

children.label: IV
children.text: an expansion of additional indoor, outdoor, or combined business space;

children.label: V
children.text: an onsite or offsite health screening capability; or

children.label: VI
children.text: other assets relating to the compliance with the requirements or guidance described in subparagraph (A), as determined by the Administrator in consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services and the Secretary of Labor; and

children.label: I
children.text: covered materials described in section 328.103(a) of title 44, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation;

children.label: II
children.text: particulate filtering facepiece respirators approved by the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health, including those approved only for emergency use authorization; or

children.label: III
children.text: other kinds of personal protective equipment, as determined by the Administrator in consultation with the Secretary of Health and Human Services and the Secretary of Labor; and

children.label: C
children.text: does not include residential real property or intangible property;
the term “eligible recipient” means the recipient of a covered loan;

children.label: A
children.text: payroll costs;

children.label: B
children.text: payments of interest on any covered mortgage obligation (which shall not include any prepayment of or payment of principal on a covered mortgage obligation);

children.label: C
children.text: payments on any covered rent obligation;

children.label: D
children.text: covered utility payments;

children.label: E
children.text: covered operations expenditures;

children.label: F
children.text: covered property damage costs;

children.label: G
children.text: covered supplier costs; and

children.label: H
children.text: covered worker protection expenditures; and

the terms “payroll costs” and “seasonal employer” have the meanings given those terms in . Such payroll costs shall not include qualified wages taken into account in determining the credit allowed under section 2301 of the CARES Act, qualified wages taken into account in determining the credit allowed under subsection (a) or (d) of section 303 of the Taxpayer Certainty and Disaster Relief Act of 2020, or premiums taken into account in determining the credit allowed under . Such payroll costs shall not include qualified wages taken into account in determining the credit allowed under subsection (a) or (d) of section 303 of the Taxpayer Certainty and Disaster Tax Relief Act of 2020.section 636(a)(36) of this titlesection 6432 of title 261So in original.1
Payroll costs.
Any payment of interest on any covered mortgage obligation (which shall not include any prepayment of or payment of principal on a covered mortgage obligation).
Any payment on any covered rent obligation.
Any covered utility payment.
Any covered operations expenditure.
Any covered property damage cost.
Any covered supplier cost.
Any covered worker protection expenditure.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Amounts which have been forgiven under this section shall be considered canceled indebtedness by a lender authorized under .section 636(a) of this title

children.heading: Purchase of guarantees
children.label: 2 Purchase of guarantees
children.text: For purposes of the purchase of the guarantee for a covered loan by the Administrator, amounts which are forgiven under this section shall be treated in accordance with the procedures that are otherwise applicable to a loan guaranteed under .section 636(a) of this title

children.heading: Remittance
children.label: 3 Remittance
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date on which the amount of forgiveness under this section is determined, the Administrator shall remit to the lender an amount equal to the amount of forgiveness, plus any interest accrued through the date of payment.

children.heading: Report
children.label: A Report
children.text: A lender authorized under , or, at the discretion of the Administrator, a third party participant in the secondary market, may, report to the Administrator an expected forgiveness amount on a covered loan or on a pool of covered loans of up to 100 percent of the principal on the covered loan or pool of covered loans, respectively.section 636(a) of this title

children.heading: Purchase
children.label: B Purchase
children.text: The Administrator shall purchase the expected forgiveness amount described in subparagraph (A) as if the amount were the principal amount of a loan guaranteed under .section 636(a) of this title

children.heading: Timing
children.label: C Timing
children.text: Not later than 15 days after the date on which the Administrator receives a report under subparagraph (A), the Administrator shall purchase the expected forgiveness amount under subparagraph (B) with respect to each covered loan to which the report relates.

children.heading: Limits on amount of forgiveness
children.label: d Limits on amount of forgiveness

children.heading: Amount may not exceed principal
children.label: 1 Amount may not exceed principal
children.text: The amount of loan forgiveness under this section shall not exceed the principal amount of the financing made available under the applicable covered loan.

children.heading: Reduction based on reduction in number of employees
children.label: 2 Reduction based on reduction in number of employees
i
children.text: the average number of full-time equivalent employees per month employed by the eligible recipient during the covered period; by

children.label: aa
children.text: the average number of full-time equivalent employees per month employed by the eligible recipient during the period beginning on and ending on ; orFebruary 15, 2019June 30, 2019

children.label: bb
children.text: the average number of full-time equivalent employees per month employed by the eligible recipient during the period beginning on and ending on ; orJanuary 1, 2020February 29, 2020

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of an eligible recipient that is seasonal employer, as determined by the Administrator, the average number of full-time equivalent employees per month employed by the eligible recipient during the period beginning on and ending on .February 15, 2019June 30, 2019

children.heading: Calculation of average number of employees
children.label: B Calculation of average number of employees
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the average number of full-time equivalent employees shall be determined by calculating the average number of full-time equivalent employees for each pay period falling within a month.

children.heading: Reduction relating to salary and wages
children.label: 3 Reduction relating to salary and wages

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The amount of loan forgiveness under this section shall be reduced by the amount of any reduction in total salary or wages of any employee described in subparagraph (B) during the covered period that is in excess of 25 percent of the total salary or wages of the employee during the most recent full quarter during which the employee was employed before the covered period.

children.heading: Employees described
children.label: B Employees described
children.text: An employee described in this subparagraph is any employee who did not receive, during any single pay period during 2019, wages or salary at an annualized rate of pay in an amount more than $100,000.

children.heading: Tipped workers
children.label: 4 Tipped workers
children.text: An eligible recipient with tipped employees described in may receive forgiveness for additional wages paid to those employees.section 203(m)(2)(A) of title 29

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In a circumstance described in subparagraph (B), the amount of loan forgiveness under this section shall be determined without regard to a reduction in the number of full-time equivalent employees of an eligible recipient or a reduction in the salary of 1 or more employees of the eligible recipient, as applicable, during the period beginning on and ending on the date that is 30 days after .February 15, 2020March 27, 2020

children.label: I
children.text: during the period beginning on and ending on the date that is 30 days after , there is a reduction, as compared to , in the number of full-time equivalent employees of an eligible recipient; andFebruary 15, 2020March 27, 2020February 15, 2020

children.label: II
children.text: not later than (or, with respect to a covered loan made on or after , not later than the last day of the covered period with respect to such covered loan), the eligible employer has eliminated the reduction in the number of full-time equivalent employees;December 31, 2020December 27, 2020

children.label: I
children.text: during the period beginning on and ending on the date that is 30 days after , there is a reduction, as compared to , in the salary or wages of 1 or more employees of the eligible recipient; andFebruary 15, 2020March 27, 2020February 15, 2020

children.label: II
children.text: not later than (or, with respect to a covered loan made on or after , not later than the last day of the covered period with respect to such covered loan), the eligible employer has eliminated the reduction in the salary or wages of such employees; orDecember 31, 2020December 27, 2020

children.label: iii
children.text: in which the events described in clause (i) and (ii) occur.

children.heading: Exemptions
children.label: 6 Exemptions
children.text: The Administrator and the Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe regulations granting de minimis exemptions from the requirements under this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: an inability to rehire individuals who were employees of the eligible recipient on ; andFebruary 15, 2020

children.label: ii
children.text: an inability to hire similarly qualified employees for unfilled positions on or before (or, with respect to a covered loan made on or after , on or before the last day of the covered period with respect to such covered loan); orDecember 31, 2020December 27, 2020

children.label: B
children.text: is able to document an inability to return to the same level of business activity as such business was operating at before , due to compliance with requirements established or guidance issued by the Secretary of Health and Human Services, the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, or the Occupational Safety and Health Administration during the period beginning on , and ending (or, with respect to a covered loan made on or after , ending on the last day of the covered period with respect to such covered loan), related to the maintenance of standards for sanitation, social distancing, or any other worker or customer safety requirement related to COVID–19.February 15, 2020March 1, 2020December 31, 2020December 27, 2020

children.heading: Limitation on forgiveness
children.label: 8 Limitation on forgiveness
children.text: To receive loan forgiveness under this section, an eligible recipient shall use at least 60 percent of the covered loan amount for payroll costs, and may use up to 40 percent of such amount for any payment of interest on any covered mortgage obligation (which shall not include any prepayment of or payment of principal on a covered mortgage obligation), any payment on any covered rent obligation, any payment on any covered operations expenditure, any payment on any covered property damage cost, any payment on any covered supplier cost, any payment on any covered worker protection expenditure, or any covered utility payment.

children.label: A
children.text: payroll tax filings reported to the Internal Revenue Service; and

children.label: B
children.text: State income, payroll, and unemployment insurance filings;

documentation, including cancelled checks, payment receipts, transcripts of accounts, purchase orders, orders, invoices, or other documents verifying payments on covered mortgage obligations, payments on covered rent obligations, payments on covered operations expenditures, payments on covered property damage costs, payments on covered supplier costs, payments on covered worker protection expenditures, and covered utility payments;

children.label: A
children.text: the documentation presented is true and correct; and

children.label: B
children.text: the amount for which forgiveness is requested was used to retain employees, make interest payments on a covered mortgage obligation, make payments on a covered rent obligation, make payments on covered operations expenditures, make payments on covered property damage costs, make payments on covered supplier costs, make payments on covered worker protection expenditures, or make covered utility payments; and
any other documentation the Administrator determines necessary.

children.heading: Prohibition on forgiveness without documentation
children.label: f Prohibition on forgiveness without documentation
children.text: No eligible recipient shall receive forgiveness under this section without submitting to the lender that is servicing the covered loan the documentation required under subsection (e) or the certification required under subsection (), as applicable.l

children.heading: Decision
children.label: g Decision
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which a lender receives an application for loan forgiveness under this section from an eligible recipient, the lender shall issue a decision on the an application.1

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 1 Definition
children.text: In this subsection, the term “initial or second draw PPP loan” means a covered loan or a loan under paragraph (37) of .section 636(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is submitted pursuant to all applicable statutory requirements, regulations, and guidance related to initial or second draw PPP loan, including under paragraph (36) or (37) of and under this section; andsection 636(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: attests that the applicant, eligible recipient, or eligible entity, as applicable, has accurately provided the certification or documentation to the lender in accordance with the statutory requirements, regulations, and guidance described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: the lender acts in good faith relating to loan origination or forgiveness of the initial or second draw PPP loan based on that reliance; and

children.label: B
children.text: all other relevant Federal, State, local, and other statutory and regulatory requirements applicable to the lender are satisfied with respect to the initial or second draw PPP loan.
no amount shall be included in the gross income of the eligible recipient by reason of forgiveness of indebtedness described in subsection (b),
no deduction shall be denied, no tax attribute shall be reduced, and no basis increase shall be denied, by reason of the exclusion from gross income provided by paragraph (1), and

children.label: A
children.text: any amount excluded from income by reason of paragraph (1) shall be treated as tax exempt income for purposes of sections 705 and 1366 of title 26, and

children.label: B
children.text: except as provided by the Secretary of the Treasury (or the Secretary’s delegate), any increase in the adjusted basis of a partner’s interest in a partnership under with respect to any amount described in subparagraph (A) shall equal the partner’s distributive share of deductions resulting from costs giving rise to forgiveness described in subsection (b).section 705 of title 26

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: j Rule of construction
children.text: The cancellation of indebtedness on a covered loan under this section shall not otherwise modify the terms and conditions of the covered loan.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: k Regulations
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , the Administrator shall issue guidance and regulations implementing this section.March 27, 2020

children.label: I
children.text: shall be not more than 1 page in length; and

children.label: aa
children.text: a description of the number of employees the eligible recipient was able to retain because of the covered loan;

children.label: bb
children.text: the estimated amount of the covered loan amount spent by the eligible recipient on payroll costs; and

children.label: cc
children.text: the total loan value;

children.label: I
children.text: accurately provided the required certification; and

children.label: II
children.text: complied with the requirements under ; andsection 636(a)(36) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to employment records, for the 4-year period following submission of the form; and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to other records, for the 3-year period following submission of the form.

children.heading: Limitation on requiring additional materials
children.label: B Limitation on requiring additional materials
children.text: An eligible recipient of a covered loan that is not more than $150,000 shall not, at the time of the application for forgiveness, be required to submit any application or documentation in addition to the certification and information required to substantiate forgiveness.

children.heading: Records for other requirements
children.label: C Records for other requirements
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (A) or (B) shall be construed to exempt an eligible recipient from having to provide documentation independently to a lender to satisfy relevant Federal, State, local, or other statutory or regulatory requirements, or in connection with an audit as authorized under subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Demographic information
children.label: D Demographic information
children.text: The certification established by the Administrator under subparagraph (A) shall include a means by which an eligible recipient may, at the discretion of the eligible recipient, submit demographic information of the owner of the eligible recipient, including the sex, race, ethnicity, and veteran status of the owner.

children.label: i
children.text: review and audit covered loans described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: ii
children.text: access any records described in subparagraph (A)(iii); and

children.label: I
children.text: the amount of a covered loan described in subparagraph (A); or

children.label: II
children.text: the loan forgiveness amount with respect to a covered loan described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to a covered loan in an amount that is more than $150,000, the eligible recipient shall submit to the lender that is servicing the covered loan the documentation described in subsection (e).

children.heading: Demographic information
children.label: B Demographic information
children.text: The process for submitting the documentation described in subsection (e) shall include a means by which an eligible recipient may, at the discretion of the eligible recipient, submit demographic information of the owner of the eligible recipient, including the sex, race, ethnicity, and veteran status of the owner.

children.label: i
children.text: the policies and procedures of the Administrator for conducting forgiveness reviews and audits of covered loans; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the metrics that the Administrator shall use to determine which covered loans will be audited.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of active reviews and audits;

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of reviews and audits that have been ongoing for more than 60 days; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any substantial changes made to the audit plan submitted under subparagraph (A).
